channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: TotalEnergies Sells Small-Scale Solar Assets in Europe
item.description: The sale is part of TotalEnergies' efforts 'to refocus its renewable development on large utility-scale solar and wind farms in order to benefit from economies of scale', the French energy giant said.

item.title: The Next Bull Market Could Be Built on Inventory Replenishment
item.description: The Middle East is once again at the center of global energy markets due to the renewed military confrontation involving Iran. Markets, however, should recognize that, unlike in previous crises, the world is entering…

item.title: Kansas Becomes Testing Ground for a Radical Nuclear Reactor Design
item.description: An unconventional approach to nuclear energy production just got one step closer to reality. A prototype reactor canister just arrived at its installation site in Kansas, where it will soon be part of a proof of concept…

item.title: The Escalating Energy War Between California and the White House
item.description: In recent years, California has grown increasingly progressive in its energy policy, shifting away from fossil fuels toward renewable alternatives. The state has invested heavily in developing its renewable energy…

item.title: Can Colombia’s New Government Reverse the Nation’s Oil and Gas Decline?
item.description: In a closely contested presidential election in Colombia, controversial far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella emerged victorious. After an official recount, his opponent, left-wing senator Ivan Cepeda, conceded…

item.title: Birol Urges EU to Reconsider Arctic Drilling Moratorium
item.description: International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol pressed the European Union to revisit its moratorium on drilling for oil and gas in the Arctic.

item.title: Can Europe Beat the Heat Without More Air Conditioners?
item.description: With Europe facing more frequent and hotter heatwaves, the uptake of air conditioning systems has soared across the region in recent years. This trend is driving energy demand and making it more difficult for countries…

item.title: IEA Says UAE Oil Output Surged to Record High in June
item.description: The country pumped 4.1 million barrels a day on average in June, the International Energy Agency said in its monthly report.

item.title: Europe’s Nuclear Plants Can’t Beat the Heat
item.description: Europe’s blistering heat wave is threatening energy security across the continent as power plants shut down and the risk of rolling blackouts rises. Electric grids are overstressed, and ecosystems are too. Rivers are…

item.title: Kazakhstan Extends Petroleum Export Ban Six Months as Hormuz Tensions Flare
item.description: Kazakhstan is growing more possessive of its petroleum products as a gasoline shortage tightens its grip in Russia and the US-Iran conflict reignites. Kazakh officials have established new police checkpoints on almost…

item.title: BOEM Explores Potential for OCS Space Launch and Recovery
item.description: 'The Outer Continental Shelf presents a significant opportunity to support the future of America's space economy', Acting BOEM Director Matt Giacona said.

item.title: Venezuela Breaks Up Oil Monopoly Under New Rules
item.description: Venezuela's interim government published long-awaited oil regulations on Thursday.

item.title: Summer Heatwaves Are Rewriting the Rules for Britain's Power Grid
item.description: The energy grid operator has warned that the “extreme temperatures” from this week’s heatwave could hit power supplies on Thursday night, as households’ use of fans and air conditioner place a record strain on our…

item.title: US Oil, Gas Drillers Hang Back in Volatile Market
item.description: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 581, up 44 from this…

item.title: Chevron to Continue Supplying Gas to Australian Utility Alinta
item.description: Chevron signed a new agreement to supply 46 petajoules of Western Australian natural gas to Alinta over five years.

item.title: China Tightens Its Grip on Namibia's Next Energy Boom
item.description: China isn't waiting for Namibia to become an oil producer. It's getting in early. During a state visit to Beijing this week, Namibia and China signed eight cooperation agreements covering energy, critical minerals…

item.title: EIA Cuts 2026, 2027 USA Gasoline Price Projection
item.description: The EIA lowered its U.S. regular gasoline price projection for both 2026 and 2027 in its latest short term energy outlook.

item.title: Oil’s Calm Is Over as Middle East Risks Return
item.description: Renewed U.S.-Iran strikes have revived the Middle East risk premium, lifting Brent above $76 as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz slows and supply disruption fears return. Friday, July 10, 2026 The return of the…

item.title: Russian Fuel Disruptions Push Central Asia Into an Energy Crisis
item.description: Ukraine's sustained attacks on Russia's oil refineries immediately plunged Central Asian countries into energy chaos, highlighting years of short-term policy decisions that have left the region without a coordinated…

item.title: IEA Chief Urges EU to Drop Arctic Drilling Ban
item.description: The European Union should reverse the current moratorium on drilling in the Arctic, where Norway is pushing to drill if allowed, Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency (IEA), has said…
